title: Sphinx configured to process docstrings
body:
* Sphinx configured to create html documentation from docstrings on API.
  Low level and deprecated classes not included in index.
* Downgraded urllib3 in setup.cfg
* Updated some docstrings after seeing the results from Sphinx

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: @lsm5 This includes the changes to setup.cfg
@jwhonce just wondering, do we need to have dependencies specified in both places? Or is it possible to keep just one?

@lsm5 The Python packaging ecosystem is in transition. Some tools still only know about `requirements.txt` while only have moved on to the new shiny new stuff. So for now we have four files... 

* requirements.txt (the development environment)
* test-requirements.txt (superset of requirements.txt)
* setup.py/setup.cfg (what is needed to install package)
* pyproject.toml (what is needed to build package, latest)

Hope this helps.

JFYI in OpenStack we use pbr which builds on top of setuptools but supports a requirements file in the setup.cfg so you don't have that duplication.  But yea, duplication is always fun.  I would argue that test-requirements.txt is not necessarily a super-set of requirements but rather what is necessary to run tests above and beyond requirements.txt. Usually it's assumed that requirements.txt is already included.

My understanding is that pyproject.toml + setup.cfg is the replacement for setup.py with newer setuptools but it's till relatively new.

@jwhonce thanks!

Can we get a new tag published?
